response.justification: Bogdan has an interesting geospatial background through GISCollective and Green Map, and demonstrated low-latency/distributed systems experience at Dunnhumby, but the core requirement for this role is deep, current C++ or Rust expertise — which is largely absent. His C++ exposure is limited to a brief 2014–2015 stint at National Instruments and a bachelor's thesis project, while his primary professional toolchain for the past decade has been TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang. There is no Rust experience, no routing or tile pipeline work, and no evidence of the systems-level C++ depth needed for a mid-senior role in geospatial routing infrastructure.
strengths: Genuine geospatial domain exposure via GISCollective (event-driven geo-data platform) and Green Map GIS application
Proven experience with high-throughput, low-latency distributed systems at Dunnhumby (millions of HTTP req/s, async I/O, fibers)
Broad systems background including Linux, Kubernetes, CI/CD, and multi-language problem solving across embedded and server contexts
concerns: C++ experience is dated (2014–2015, NI LabWindows and a thesis project) with no evidence of modern C++ (C++17/20) at commercial scale
No Rust experience and no routing/navigation pipeline or tile-processing work anywhere in the resume
Current role and recent trajectory (TypeScript, Node.js, AI/MCP tooling) points away from systems programming, making a fit for this C++-primary role unlikely without significant re-skilling
